name: Portlight v1.0.0
body: First stable release of Portlight — a trade-first maritime strategy CLI.
10 ports across 3 regions, 8 trade goods, 17 sea routes
Route arbitrage, contracts, infrastructure, finance, and commercial reputation
Save/load with full state round-trip
609 tests passing
pip:**

name: v1.4.7
body: Fix:** Treaty counter derived from game state instead of module-level global (prevents duplicate treaty IDs across game loads)
Fix:** SECURITY.md corrected wallet filename and proof file description
Fix:** Wallet seed no longer printed to terminal in `sov wallet` output
Fix:** `_load_game()` return type no longer uses `type: ignore` suppression
Changed:** PyPI classifier updated to Production/Stable
Changed:** Combined publish + release-binaries into single `release.yml` workflow

name: v1.0.2
body: Version bump to allow CI trusted publisher to handle PyPI upload (v1.0.1 was uploaded manually, causing the CI publish to 400).
No code changes — same content as v1.0.1.

name: v1.0.1
body: Security fix**: Cross-platform path validation now correctly detects Windows drive roots (previously missed by hardcoded path set)
Publish CLI**: `hwb publish` command wired into CLI (backend existed, CLI was missing)
Git-aware versioning**: `hwb version-next --path .` analyzes conventional commits to suggest semver bumps
PEP 561**: Added `py.typed` marker for type hint support
Test coverage**: +39 tests across 6 new test files (895 total), covering security scanner, changelog generator, notify sender, cache registry, publish CLI, and git-aware version-next
Code cleanup**: Consolidated duplicate path validation logic, fixed test warnings (empty match strings, unawaited coroutines, blind exception catches)

name: v1.1.0
body: Rust CLI** — detects `Cargo.toml`, custom roles for benches/examples
Go CLI** — detects `go.mod`, custom roles for internal packages/proto
.NET CLI** — detects `*.csproj`, custom roles for project files/migrations
Java CLI** — detects `pom.xml`/`build.gradle`, custom roles for resources/proto
Profiles can now define domain-specific roles with glob patterns and configurable confidence scores. Custom roles are evaluated at priority 14, after all built-in roles.
Compare two `META_ZIP_INDEX.json` files to find structural similarities:

name: v1.1.0
body: Sounds now evolve with your session — not just what tool fired, but how the work is *going*.
Streak awareness** — consecutive successful plays build harmonic intensity (5 levels). Your productive session *sounds* productive.
Error escalation** — repeated errors get progressively more urgent (5 levels). You'll know something is stuck without reading the output.
Completion fanfare** — session end is outcome-aware: triumphant ascending chord for great sessions, muted resolution for rough ones.
New `--intensity` and `--escalation` flags for manual control
`demo` and `export` commands showcase all new variants

name: v1.2.0 — Preview Reliability
body: Two new MCP tools that solve the preview race condition and false-positive hook problem:
`guardian_preview_ready`** — Polls a localhost port until the dev server responds. Use between `preview_start` and `preview_snapshot` to avoid `chrome-error://` race conditions.

`guardian_preview_recover`** — Diagnoses stuck preview sessions. Classifies the project type (web vs desktop vs CLI) and returns step-by-step recovery guidance. For non-web projects, returns "skip preview" to suppress the irrelevant hook.
Port readiness probe** (`src/port-probe.ts`) — TCP/HTTP polling with configurable interval, timeout, and health check. Zero new dependencies.
Project classifier** (`src/project-classify.ts`) — Heuristic detection of web-node, web-python, web-static, desktop (Tauri/MAUI/Electron/WPF), and CLI projects.
Recovery plan includes preview readiness guidance at healthy level

name: v1.0.1
body: DaemonVersion now correctly reports the current version instead of `0.2.0-lite`
Event log file no longer grows unbounded — rotates at 5 MB, keeps one generation
Event writes are buffered (flush every 500ms or 50 entries) instead of synchronous per-event
Event stream endpoint no longer holds the global lock during long-poll
Tray app resolves control panel path for packaged layout (sibling `app/` folder)
Tray polls notification settings every 30s instead of every 2s

name: v1.0.2
body: CI stability: isolated smoke test experiment stores, added test timeouts, reduced stderr buffer test data size

name: v1.1.0
body: `start` skips completed lessons and picks up where you left off. Shows "Welcome back" when resuming.
Guided flow asks "What do you want to write on the ledger?" before lesson 4 — the moment people remember.
Each lesson tracks duration. Total training time shown in certificate, proof pack, and completion banner.
`xrpl-camp status` shows a rich visual checklist with ✓/◌ markers, durations, and a "← next" indicator.
110 tests (19 new), all passing
Fully backward-compatible with existing session files

name: v1.0.1
body: Training metrics tracking**: `lastStep` and `lastLoss` saved on every progress update, displayed in `backprop list`
Enhanced list command**: Shows run duration, training step, loss, and checkpoint count
Checkpoint existence validation**: Auto-resume verifies checkpoint files exist on disk
Corrupted DB backup**: Timestamped `.corrupt` backup created before starting fresh
stderr buffer limit**: Python stderr capped at 10 MB to prevent memory exhaustion
CLI version mismatch**: `backprop --version` now reads from `package.json` dynamically

name: GlyphStudio v1 — A Governed Pixel Asset Studio
body: GlyphStudio v1 is a governed pixel asset studio for creating stills, animation, variants, reusable parts, and structured output.
This release focuses on five connected product arcs:
Palette variants, reusable parts, document variants, and batch bundle export.
Unified library, pinning and recency, search and keyboard retrieval, and interchange.
Project templates, asset packs, apply-to-project flows, and an upgraded start surface.
Starter recipes, contextual workflow hints, shortcut discovery, and canonical sample content.

name: v1.0.0
body: First stable release.
Compile repos into structured `llms.txt` Markdown documents
Auto-sync to Google Drive with idempotent file IDs
Watch mode with configurable debounce
MCP server for AI assistant integration (sync_to_drive, compile_context, sync_status, list_repos)
Smart filtering via `.gitignore`, `.llmsignore`, and built-in rules

name: Soundweave v1.0.1
body: Adaptive soundtrack studio for composing, arranging, scoring, and exporting interactive game music. Soundweave is a composition-first, adaptation-aware workstation — game music that feels intentional, not generated.
Studio** — 13-screen authoring UI for clips, stems, scenes, bindings, transitions, automation, and score maps
16 packages** — Schema, audio engine, clip engine, music theory, score map, automation, library, runtime pack, and more
Adaptive logic** — Trigger bindings, deterministic scene resolution, crossfade/stinger/cooldown transitions
World scoring** — Motif families, score profiles, cue families, derivation chains
Sample workflow** — Import, trim, slice, kit builder, sample instruments

name: v1.0.4
body: Starlight handbook (7 pages, 7 locales), retranslated READMEs, landing page CTA → handbook.

name: v1.0.3
body: httpx connection pooling** — reuse connections instead of creating one per request
Semaphore context manager** — `async with sem:` pattern, prevents slot leaks on exceptions
Race condition fix** — `_active` counter protected by asyncio.Lock
Cache path traversal** — uses `relative_to()` instead of string comparison
Structured logging** — replaces `print(stderr)` with Python logging module
Specific exceptions** — no more bare `except Exception` in ollama.py

name: v1.0.2
body: Renamed PyPI distribution from `py-polyglot` to `polyglot-gpu` (import name `pypolyglot` unchanged)
CLI entry point renamed to `polyglot-gpu`
Added trusted publisher workflow for PyPI releases
```bash
pip install polyglot-gpu

body: Oregon Trail-style survival game with AI Game Master and optional XRPL ledger backpack.
Procedurally generated wilderness with seeded world generation
6 camp actions: travel, rest, hunt, repair, ration, abandon cargo
AI Game Master with 3 narrative profiles (Chronicler, Fireside, Lantern-Bearer)

Added LICENSE to all sub-packages for npm compliance
Complete translations: Japanese, Chinese, Spanish, French, Hindi, Italian, Portuguese

name: v1.7.0 — Supply Chain Receipts
body: Three new receipt pipelines for security teams and executives:
registry-sync** — Prove what's published vs what's in the repo
Drift classification: in sync, ahead, behind, unpublished
Each package becomes a receipt output with version comparison
security-audit** — Prove what you scanned, with what tool, and what was found
Severity breakdown (critical/high/moderate/low/info) in intent and metadata

name: v1.6.0 — Bundle Authority
body: Bundles can now be signed and their signatures verified, establishing **who created the parcel**. The zip contents are never mutated — signatures live as detached sidecars (`.sig`/`.cert`) next to the bundle.
`rf bundle sign <file>`** — Sign a bundle with cosign (detached sidecar)
`--keyless` for OIDC-based signing in CI
`--key <path>` for local key-based signing
`rf bundle verify --require-bundle-signature`** — Require a valid bundle-level signature
`rf verify <file.zip> --require-bundle-signature`** — Auto-detect passthrough

name: v1.5.0 — Receipt Bundles
body: v1.5.0 introduces **receipt bundles** — self-verifying zip archives that package a receipt with its entire trust chain into a single portable file.
bundle.zip/
├── manifest.json     # Bundle metadata
├── hashes.json       # SHA-256 of every file
├── VERIFY.md         # Human-readable verification instructions

name: v1.4.0 — Strict Refs, Policy Identity, Policy Signing
body: Default stays best-effort — missing references are reported but don't fail verification. With `--refs-strict`, missing or unreadable references fail. Hash mismatches always fail regardless of mode.
```bash
rf verify receipt.json --follow --refs-strict --receipts-dir receipts/

Receipts now embed `policy_identity.hash` — the SHA-256 of the lint policy rules applied at creation time. During verification, if a policy is supplied, the hash is compared. Mismatch = fail. This prevents policy swapping.
`PolicyIdentity` type: `hash`, optional `version`, `path`, `signed`, `signatureRef`

name: v1.3.0 — Multi-Tenant SaaS Architecture
body: App template upgraded** to `/app/[workspace]/…` multi-tenant routing (3 seeded workspaces)
RBAC policy layer** — `canViewBilling`, `canManageTeam`, `canManageWorkspace`
Feature flags** — `billing`, `teams`, `auditLog`, `apiKeys` with `isEnabled()` gating
Centralized data layer** — workspace-keyed projects, metrics, and activity
Path-preserving workspace switcher** with plan badges (starter / pro / business)
AccessDenied component** + billing page RBAC gate

name: v1.0.1 — Handbook + Publish Workflow
body: HANDBOOK.md** — complete operational reference (13 chapters + 3 appendices) covering architecture, commands, performance model, Docker execution, resource governance, security, tuning, troubleshooting, and design decisions
Publish workflow** — 3-channel automated release (PyPI via OIDC, GHCR gateway image, GitHub Release artifacts)
```bash
pip install --upgrade claude-toolstack-cli

name: v1.0.0 — Bounded Code Intelligence
body: A ready-to-deploy stack that keeps Claude Code productive on large, multi-language repositories without thrashing a 64-GB Linux workstation.
FastAPI gateway** with 6 bounded endpoints (search, slice, ctags, symbol, job runner, metrics)
systemd cgroup v2 slices** — 5 slices enforce MemoryHigh/Max per service group
Exec-only Docker model** — socket proxy blocks 14 higher-risk Docker endpoints
`cts` CLI** — zero-dependency Python client with text/JSON/Claude output modes
Evidence bundles v2** — 4 bundle modes (default, error, symbol, change) with path ranking + git recency
